    Maternal-Fetal Immunology

    Why doesn’t the maternal immune system reject the fetus?

    Start Learning

    Part 1: Immunology Foundations

    Self vs. non-self, innate vs. adaptive immunity, and how the body learns not to attack itself.

    Read

    Part 2: The Puzzle of Pregnancy

    Why the fetus isn’t rejected, and how the uterus and placenta actively negotiate tolerance.

    Read

    Part 3: Model Systems

    How cancer and autoimmunity illuminate the fundamental mechanisms of immune tolerance.

    Read

    Part 4: When Tolerance Breaks Down

    Recurrent miscarriage, preeclampsia, and preterm birth — the immunological roots of obstetric complications.

    Read

    Part 5: Applications Beyond Pregnancy

    What pregnancy teaches us about cancer, transplant medicine, and autoimmune disease.

    Read

    Part 6: Frontiers & Why We Need You

    Open questions, cutting-edge tools, and how to get involved in maternal-fetal immunology research.

    Read
